topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: A collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: Who is The Book of Secret Knowledge aimed at?
entries.a: System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: How can I contribute to The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: By making a pull request with valid and well-reasoned explanations about your changes or comments.

entries.q: What type of content is included in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, Systems/Services, Networks, Containers/Orchestration, Manuals/Howtos/Tutorials, and more.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the RSS/Atom feed in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: To keep users informed about all changes and updates.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI Tools included in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, Oh My ZSH!, and more.

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins included in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting, and more.

entries.q: What are some examples of managers included in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: Midnight Commander, ranger, nnn, and more.
